hypothesis: Introducing artificial neural variability in the training process of deep neural networks can mitigate catastrophic forgetting by improving the robustness and generalizability of the models.
minimum_experiment:
MECHANISM: Artificial neural variability reduces overfitting in deep neural networks. VARIABLE: V = accuracy()
INTERVENTION: Technique of artificial neural variability vs baseline of no variability. Simulated as: generate data using numpy/scipy with Gaussian distribution
MEASUREMENT: Metric: V, computed as average accuracy on unseen data
SUCCESS CRITERION: V exceeds baseline by >0.1
CONTROL: 1-2 confounders held constant: batch size and learning rate, held constant at 32 and 0.001 respectively

issues: The experiment does not directly test the mechanism of artificial neural variability reducing catastrophic forgetting
The use of synthetic data may not accurately represent real-world scenarios

domain_blocked: - Graph Theory -> Remote Sensing Object Detection
- Social Network Analysis -> Citation Network Analysis
- Array Programming -> Telescope Array Data Analysis
